titlestmt.titleproper: Hospital Annual Reports and Ephemera Collection
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 5, 2020 at 03:25

did.unittitle: Hospital annual reports and ephemera collection

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: Annual or biennial reports and other pamphlets issued by hospitals in China, Great Britain (especially London), and the United States. The states best represented are Massachusetts, New York, and Pennsylvania. Types of hospitals include general hospitals, specialty hospitals (children's, eye, lying-in, etc.), dispensaries, asylums, and homeopathic hospitals. About 200 hospitals are represented in this collection.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hospital annual reports and ephemera collection. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was created by the Medical Historical Library. Provenance for individual items in the collection is unknown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in series and subseries alphabetically by country, state, city, and hospital. Items in hospital subseries are arranged by date.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The city of Peking is currently known as Beijing.

Sanatorium for Consumption and Diseases of the Chest
Money, Walter. Outline of the History of Maison Dieu
Fareham Union Royal Sanitary Isolation Hospital
Reports of the visiting justices, quarter sessions, for 1828-1839
Asylum for the Cure Scrofula & Glandular Diseases upon the Medical Principles of Mr. Charles Whitlaw
City of London Asylum for Diseases of the Chest
Dr. John Francis Churchill's Free Stoechiological Dispensatory for Consumption…
Letter...on the resignation of the medical officers
Hospital for Diseased and Sick Seamen
Programme of the Proceedings...on Laying the Foundation Stone…

scopecontent.p: Includes diet card.

did.unittitle: Queen Charlotte's Lying-In Hospital, St. Marylebone
St. Mary's, Haggerston Lying in Charity
Samaritan Free Hospital for Women and Children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly Verral Spinal Hospital.

did.unittitle: Hospital of St. Mary the Virgin
Case prepared on behalf of the St. Bartholomew's committee

West-Riding of York Pauper Lunatic Asylum
Lunatic Asylum for the North and East Ridings of Yorkshire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commonly known as the York Retreat.

did.unittitle: Institution for the Relief of Persons Labouring Under Incurable Disease

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also: Material filed with Yale-New Haven Hospital in "Archives" and material catalogued by LC number and in the stacks.

Illinois Charitable Eye and Ear Infirmary
Illinois Eastern Hospital for the Insane

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as Kankakee State Hospital (1910-1974) and Shapiro Developmental Center (1974--).

did.unittitle: Outline used in conducting and recording the examination of

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly Kentucky Lunatick Asylum (1844-1849).

did.unittitle: Robert Garret Free Hospitals for Children, The

Sale in aid of the Children's Hospital
A Summary of the Work it has Accomplished since 1870
60th and 61st annual report, part 1 and 2, for 1928 and 1929
Collis P. Huntington Memorial Hospital for Cancer Research and Laboratories of the Cancer Commission of Harvard University

Murdock Free Surgical Hospital for Women
New England Hospital for Women and Children

scopecontent.p: Also known as State Lunatic Hospital and Northampton Lunatic Hospital.

scopecontent.p: Includes Michigan Asylum for the Insane.

scopecontent.p: Also New Hampshire Asylum.

scopecontent.p: Includes extracts of addresses by James H. Armsby.

scopecontent.p: Also known as Western Dispensary.

scopecontent.p: Includes Cornell Medical College

New York Skin and Cancer Hospital
New York Throat and Nose Hospital, with eye and ear department and separate wards for consumptives

scopecontent.p: Includes New York Nursery and Child's Hospital.

did.unittitle: Consolidation of the Nursery and Child's Hospital and the New York Infant Asylum under the name of the New York Nursery and Child's Hospital

scopecontent.p: Includes Sloane Maternity Hospital.

Opening, with address by Edward Delafield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as S.R. Smith Infirmary.

did.unittitle: State Hospital of the City of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May have been given to Henry Farnam with Woman's Hospital in the State of New York, 1855-1911.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes New York Woman's Infirmary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With 4th annual report of the Pennsylvania Free Dispensary.

scopecontent.p: Also known as Wills Eyes Hospital and Wills Hospital.

did.unittitle: Butler Hospital for the Insane/for the Treatment of Nervous and Mental Illnesses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Name shortened to Butler Hospital in 1900s.

did.unittitle: Ruggles, Arthur H. The Patient, the Hospital and You.
Address at the opening of the Pathological Laboratory
